pages.raw_text:
Prof. J. A. L. Waddell, the Kan.
sas City bridge expert, is here
again, evidently to expert the Mon
roe st. bridge. This afternoon he is
holding a conference with General
Manager Hunting of the w. w. P.
Co. at the Spokane club. The city
did not send for Mr. Waddell.


The Monroe st. bridge was
months ago condemned by Expert
Strauss of Chicago, and 1t was the
intention tot construct new
bridge of reinforced concrete. Just
what Prof. Waddell's presence
means is something of mystery.
Nominally he is the guest of City
Engineer Ralston. But those who
claim an insight into the real rea-
son for the experts presence here
say it was at the instigation of Of
ficials of the Washington Water
Power Co., who desire that Mon.
roe st. bridge, instead of being re.
built of concrete, be patched up. To
that end 1t is necessary to have
still another expert" opinion as
to it's advisability--and, therefore,
the expert has been sent for and
will render his opinion, probably at
the expense of the Washington
Water Power Co.


The city paid more than $1,000
for Straus' report, which condemn.
ed the structure. An o. R. & N.
expert, before Straus came, "ex-
Pcited" some of the bridge, but
SHE'D when asked to expert the
Monroe st. structure. He let the
city promising to return and tackle
the Monroe st. question In a month.
He has not been back since.


The city has desired that the
Washington Water Power Co. pay
one half of the proposed $4,000
expense of constructing new
bridge. Perhaps 1t can be patch
ed up few years for one half that
expense. Thus the w. w. p. would
not be required to expend so much
money. 1t might agree to paying
for half of the patchwork, but II
patched. according to Mr.. Straus, it
would not last long and eventually
a new bridge would be required.


The successful floatation of a
100000 bridge bond issue has
been accomplished. 1t was thought
that the greater portion of this
